mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS below 0.25% suppresses the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and pushes investors out the risk curve into equities, producing a structural positive drift in S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as long as the ZIRP regime persists.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60-day returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or SPX exhibits negative mean forward returns during sustained sub-0.25% FEDFUNDS periods.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals accelerating labor market healing that reduces recession probability and supports risk-asset re-rating, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=8.2 at t-1mo, declining from cycle peak
failure_condition: In more than 40% of historical instances where the trigger fires, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates a broadening consumer and housing-led demand recovery that is not captured by either series alone, since housing starts lead durable goods demand while retail sales confirm current consumption strength; this combined signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=698 at t-2mo recovering from post-crisis lows
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show forward 20-day returns not statistically distinguishable from single-series-only signals (p > 0.10), or Rank IC of the joint signal is below that of RSAFS alone over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trailing 3-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and forward earnings momentum for cyclical S&P 500 constituents, producing a short-horizon positive drift in equity returns as the market incrementally prices in improving corporate output.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=96.2095 at t-2mo, above prior readings
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing INDPRO trend and forward 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly PAYEMS gains above 150,000 signal durable job creation that reinforces consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations; this effect is materially stronger during early-cycle recoveries following a recession than in mature expansions, producing outsized positive quarterly S&P 500 returns specifically in the post-recession regime.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=132821 thousand at t-1mo, still below pre-crisis peak
regime_hint: early post-recession expansion with labor market slack
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-return relationship shows no significant difference (t-test p > 0.05) in slope between early-cycle and late-cycle subsamples, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window in the early-cycle regime.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above 2.5%, markets price in higher probability of earlier Fed tightening from the current near-zero FEDFUNDS stance, raising discount rate expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
regime_hint: ZIRP regime with rising inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDPC1 growth above trend and rising RSAFS confirm that aggregate demand expansion is both broad-based (GDP) and consumer-driven (retail sales), reinforcing corporate earnings growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; neither series alone reliably captures this combined confirmation effect.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS 3-month growth > 1%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger 60-day forward returns fail to exceed the unconditional SPX mean return by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Under a ZIRP regime (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%), short-horizon equity returns become unusually sensitive to incremental labor market surprises because rate-based valuation anchors are absent, so a monthly UNRATE decline acts as the dominant near-term catalyst for SPX gains; this sensitivity is muted or absent once FEDFUNDS rises materially above zero.
failure_condition: UNRATE-return sensitivity (regression slope) in ZIRP subsample is not statistically distinguishable from non-ZIRP subsample (p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window within ZIRP period.
